Dinner Fellowship with Serenity Inns Residents
Take a meal to the men at Serenity Inns and stay to enjoy the meal with them. They share their
stories with the group while experiencing valuable social interactions which were often lost during
addiction. This is a great activity to do with your middle and high school-aged youth so they can hear
Serenity Inns provides a safe home for men in Milwaukee to recover from their addiction to drugs or alcohol. FPLC
volunteers provide 3 dinners a month for the residents. You can also drop off a meal or order a meal to be delivered.
To sign up click the button to the right. Do you have questions? Lorraine Buehler, FPLC member & long-time
Serenity Inns volunteer can help! Email Lorraine at lcbuehler@cs.com.
Some of these days fill up very quickly, so book ahead! Fridays: August 20, September 17,
October 15, November 19, December 17; Wednesdays: August 25, November 24, December 22

The in-person Celebration of Recovery Celebration of Recovery

This one-hour, class is a wonderful way for parents and their babies from birth to 6 months old to connect. Be
prepared to Zoom—all you need is yourself in comfy clothes, your baby(s) and a blanket.
You'll be taught the head-to-toe infant massage techniques that parents need to comfort, reduce irritability, help
baby sleep faster and sleep longer, and to overall connect with baby. Please contact Angie Biersach, FPLC member,
parent, and certified infant massage instructor with questions or to RSVP for the class (see contact info below).
Dates: Aug 20, Sept 17, Oct 22, Nov 19, Dec 17
Instructor: Angie Biersach, MA, CCLS, CFLE, CIMI; angiebiersach@gmail.com or 414-207-2808

This devotion, written by FPLC member
"If I speak in the tongues of men or of angels,
but do not have love, I am only a resounding
gong, or a clanging cymbal. “ – 1 Corinthians 13
I enjoy container gardening. The color, the
pots and the variety, it’s all such fun! I’d asked
my husband to water our flowers using the
gentle setting on the sprayer, so that the
potting soil wouldn’t splash back. When he
came in the house he was truthful about it; he said he’d used the stream setting, the one he
believes is more efficient without hurting the flowers. I believe it’s quite powerful for fragile
plantings. To be fair, his way isn’t wrong; it’s just not my way. Talk about angry…it was me…he
had disobeyed my orders! This relationship is 50/50 and I’d forgotten! But then I stepped back.
He had done the job I’d asked of him, he was honest about it and the flowers and plants were
How ungrateful I was that day, what a lack of understanding on my part! How much I needed
to turn myself around. We recently celebrated our 37th anniversary and whether it’s the 1st
year or the 50th, the equation is the same; the end result must be love and for it to be love
there must first be love. This required some quieting and calming words from Our Heavenly
“Let the morning bring me word of your unfailing love, for I have put my trust in you. Show me the
way I should go, for to you I entrust my life.” – Psalm 143:8
But what made me turn it around? Not so long ago, I might have held on to that trivial anger
and made that episode a flashpoint for an argument. But I didn’t do that. All the seeds planted
over the years from our amazing church home, Fox Point Lutheran kept me grounded.
Listening and pondering powerful sermons from Pastor Bill and Pastor Bruce, following this
year’s children’s ministry from Julie, attending small group discussions with wonderful leaders
about sometimes difficult issues, reading scripture and keeping my prayer life active pointed
the way towards what God is…and what He desires in us...Love…God’s greatest command!
“Dear God, As you draw me ever deeper into your heart, I discover that my companions on the
journey are women and men loved by you as fully and as intimately as I am. In your compassionate
heart, there is a place for all of them. No one is excluded. Give me a share in your compassion dear
God, so that your unlimited love may become visible in the way I love my brothers and sisters.
Thank you for loving us, Father and let our love be visible! – Mary
